revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuwashi-hime]][[fa:کوواشی هیمه]][[ja:細媛命]][[ko:구와시히메노미코토]][[zh:細媛命]]
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Kōrei|qid=Q313041}}|children={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|qid=Q333572}}|father={{ill|Q97496325|qid=Q97496325}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11605816]]
Wikidata name: Empress Kuwashihime
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Short description|Japanese empress}}
{{Nihongo|'''Kuwashi-hime no Mikoto'''|細媛命|4=also read as Hoso-hime no Mikoto}},<ref>According to the Hosohime no Mikoto section of the Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names, Popular Edition (Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 2010).</ref> was the empress of [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]. She is believed to be the birth mother of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]].

== Overview ==
In the text of the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (The Chronicles of Japan), she is said to be the daughter of Omoku, the lord of Isogi Prefecture, while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (The Records of Ancient Matters) says she was Hosohime, the daughter of Omoku, the founder of Juichi Prefecture.

In the first book of the ''Nihon Shoki'', the empress of Emperor Koryō was Kasuga no Chihayamakahime, and in the second book it was Mashitahime, the ancestor of the Juichi prefectures.

== Related historical documents ==

* Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, The Ancient Japanese Biographical Dictionary (Yoshikawa Kobunkan).
* "A Genealogical Directory of the Emperor's Family" (Shinninjin Oraisha).

== family tree ==
{{Generations of Jimmu}}

==Notelist==
{{notelist}}

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

| after = {{ill|Utsushikome|ja|欝色謎命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Utsushikome|qq=}}
| years = 289–214 BC
}}

}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuwashi-hime]][[fa:کوواشی هیمه]][[ja:細媛命]][[ko:구와시히메노미코토]][[zh:細媛命]]
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Kōrei|WD=Q313041}}|children={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572}}|father={{ill|Q97496325|WD=Q97496325}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11605816]]
Wikidata name: Empress Kuwashihime
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Short description|Japanese empress}}
{{Nihongo|'''Kuwashi-hime no Mikoto'''|細媛命|4=also read as Hoso-hime no Mikoto}},<ref>According to the Hosohime no Mikoto section of the Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names, Popular Edition (Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 2010).</ref> was the empress of [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]. She is believed to be the birth mother of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]].

== Overview ==
In the text of the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (The Chronicles of Japan), she is said to be the daughter of Omoku, the lord of Isogi Prefecture, while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (The Records of Ancient Matters) says she was Hosohime, the daughter of Omoku, the founder of Juichi Prefecture.

In the first book of the ''Nihon Shoki'', the empress of Emperor Koryō was Kasuga no Chihayamakahime, and in the second book it was Mashitahime, the ancestor of the Juichi prefectures.

== Related historical documents ==

* Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, The Ancient Japanese Biographical Dictionary (Yoshikawa Kobunkan).
* "A Genealogical Directory of the Emperor's Family" (Shinninjin Oraisha).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuwashi-hime]][[fa:کوواشی هیمه]][[ja:細媛命]][[ko:구와시히메노미코토]][[zh:細媛命]]
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038}}|spouse={{ill|Kōrei|WD=Q313041}}|children={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572}}|father={{ill|Q97496325|WD=Q97496325}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11605816]]
Wikidata name: Empress Kuwashihime
[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:47, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Short description|Japanese empress}}
{{Nihongo|'''Kuwashi-hime no Mikoto'''|細媛命|4=also read as Hoso-hime no Mikoto}},<ref>According to the Hosohime no Mikoto section of the Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names, Popular Edition (Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 2010).</ref> was the empress of [[:en:Emperor Kōrei|Emperor Kōrei]]. She is believed to be the birth mother of [[:en:Emperor Kōgen|Emperor Kōgen]].

== Overview ==
In the text of the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (The Chronicles of Japan), she is said to be the daughter of Omoku, the lord of Isogi Prefecture, while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (The Records of Ancient Matters) says she was Hosohime, the daughter of Omoku, the founder of Juichi Prefecture.

In the first book of the ''Nihon Shoki'', the empress of Emperor Koryō was Kasuga no Chihayamakahime, and in the second book it was Mashitahime, the ancestor of the Juichi prefectures.

== Related historical documents ==

* Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, The Ancient Japanese Biographical Dictionary (Yoshikawa Kobunkan).
* "A Genealogical Directory of the Emperor's Family" (Shinninjin Oraisha).

{{Nihongo|'''Kuwashi-hime no Mikoto'''|細媛命|4=also read as Hoso-hime no Mikoto}},<ref>According to the Hosohime no Mikoto section of the Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names, Popular Edition (Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 2010).</ref> was the empress of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei}}. She is believed to be the birth mother of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen}}.

== Overview ==
In the text of the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (The Chronicles of Japan), she is said to be the daughter of Omoku, the lord of Isogi Prefecture, while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (The Records of Ancient Matters) says she was Hosohime, the daughter of Omoku, the founder of Juichi Prefecture.

In the first book of the ''Nihon Shoki'', the empress of Emperor Koryō was Kasuga no Chihayamakahime, and in the second book it was Mashitahime, the ancestor of the Juichi prefectures.

== Related historical documents ==

* Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, The Ancient Japanese Biographical Dictionary (Yoshikawa Kobunkan).
* "A Genealogical Directory of the Emperor's Family" (Shinninjin Oraisha).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuwashi-hime]][[fa:کوواشی هیمه]][[ja:細媛命]][[ko:구와시히메노미코토]][[zh:細媛命]]
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Kuwashi-hime]][[fa:کوواشی هیمه]][[ja:細媛命]][[ko:구와시히메노미코토]][[zh:細媛命]]
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|WD=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Kōrei|WD=Q313041|1=Emperor Kōrei}}|children={{ill|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|1=Emperor Kōgen}}|father={{ill|Q97496325|WD=Q97496325|1=Toichi no Agatanushi Ōme}}}}
{{Short description|Japanese empress}}
{{Nihongo|'''Kuwashi-hime no Mikoto'''|細媛命|4=also read as Hoso-hime no Mikoto}},<ref>According to the Hosohime no Mikoto section of the Dictionary of Ancient Japanese Clans and Personal Names, Popular Edition (Yoshikawa Kobunkan, 2010).</ref> was the empress of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|WD=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}. She is believed to be the birth mother of {{ill|Emperor Kōgen|en|Emperor Kōgen|WD=Q333572|lt=Emperor Kōgen|1=Emperor Kōgen}}.

== Overview ==
In the text of the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' (The Chronicles of Japan), she is said to be the daughter of Omoku, the lord of Isogi Prefecture, while the ''[[Kojiki]]'' (The Records of Ancient Matters) says she was Hosohime, the daughter of Omoku, the founder of Juichi Prefecture.

In the first book of the ''Nihon Shoki'', the empress of Emperor Koryō was Kasuga no Chihayamakahime, and in the second book it was Mashitahime, the ancestor of the Juichi prefectures.

== Related historical documents ==

* Taro Sakamoto and Kunio Hirano, The Ancient Japanese Biographical Dictionary (Yoshikawa Kobunkan).
* "A Genealogical Directory of the Emperor's Family" (Shinninjin Oraisha).
